data.content:
Plan for single-canonical-form refactor (foolery-b42b):

DATA-SHAPE DECISION
- Per-provider extractors emit display-form: Claude returns {provider:"Claude", model:"Claude", flavor:"Opus", version:"4.7"}; Codex returns {provider:"Codex", model:"GPT" or "ChatGPT", flavor:"Codex Mini"|"Codex"|"Codex Spark"|"Codex Max"|"Mini"|undefined, version}; Gemini returns {provider:"Gemini", model:"Gemini", flavor:"Pro"/"Flash"/"Flash Lite"(opt -preview), version}.
- OpenCode (preferred): model = pre-formatted display string ("OpenRouter MoonshotAI Kimi-k"), flavor = undefined, version split off ("2.6"). Vocabulary table for known compound vendor names (openrouter, moonshotai, anthropic, z-ai, mistral, google) plus auto-uppercase trailing AI/ML/IO/JS suffix; default capitalize-first-letter; version-split rule = first numeric run after model name (carry over the existing trailing-numeric joiner from foolery-988b).
- Lease stamping: lease_model carries display-form composite "Codex Mini/GPT" (provider-and-flavor join unchanged in shape, but display-cased). Tests updated lock-step.
- The previous lower-case canonical join "codex-mini/gpt" -> "Codex Mini/GPT". Round-trip test asserts display-form survives create -> show.

STEPS
1. agent-identity.ts: rewrite normalize{Claude,Codex,Gemini,Copilot,OpenCode}Model to emit display-form. Add formatOpenCodePathSegments helper with vocabulary + version-split. Delete MODEL_LABELS, formatModelDisplay, formatFlavorDisplay (or stub formatModelDisplay to identity for back-compat callers). Simplify formatAgentFamily to single filter+join. parseAgentDisplayParts: rebuild OpenCode path label using new segment formatter; pills order unchanged.
2. agent-identity-canonical.ts: confirm derivedLeaseModel still joins flavor+model with "/" but now both are display-cased. No code change unless join is now wrong (only OpenCode case which still uses path; verify).
3. settings-toml-migrate.ts (new file, <500 lines): one-time migration. Detect legacy machine-form (model in known machine vocab e.g. "gpt"/"claude"/"opus"/lowercase opencode router segment via flavor match). Re-extract and re-stamp. Idempotent: if already in display form, no-op.
4. Tests: update src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity.test.ts, agent-identity-opencode.test.ts, agent-identity-display-parts.test.ts, knots-canonical-metadata.test.ts, knots-lease-roundtrip.test.ts. Add new test for OpenCode segment formatter.
5. Contract doc (docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md): new section "Single canonical form" + new self-audit grep "MODEL_LABELS[".
6. Run quality gates: lint, tsc, test, build. Fix test breakage caused by display-form output (many tests will).
7. Commit; rebase main; push origin/main; advance through plan_review/implementation/implementation_review/shipment/shipment_review.

RISK / SURPRISES
- Many tests assert lowercase ("opus", "gpt", "claude/opus", etc.). Will need updating in lock-step.
- agent-config-normalization.normalizeRegisteredAgentConfig: model field is the runtime model id (claude-opus-4-7) not the family token. Untouched.
- canonicalizeRuntimeModel: still emits runtime ids; untouched.
- buildAgentOptionId / displayCommandLabel: unaffected (already produces display labels).
- approval-escalations-panel uses formatAgentDisplayLabel({label: approval.agentName}) which goes through the label fallback path, not extraction. Should still work.

OUT OF SCOPE
- Active leases at deploy: short-lived; no explicit reconciliation.
- Historical step_history JSONL records: left alone per knot description.

TEST STRATEGY
- Per-provider extractor tests: assert display-form outputs.
- knots-canonical-metadata: createLease argv carries Codex/Codex/Codex Mini/GPT/5.5 etc.; OpenCode argv carries the new pre-formatted display model.
- knots-lease-roundtrip: round-trip survives display-form.
- Add new test for OpenCode segment formatter cases listed in AC-6.
- Migration test: legacy machine-form input -> display-form output, idempotent.
